Definition: The client shall establish a TCP/IP connection to the server before any data exchange is attempted.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The client shall send a connection verification request before issuing any operational command.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: The client shall allow the user to request current aircraft sensor data from the server
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The client shall allow the user to request a telemetry log file from the server.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The client shall display received sensor data into readable user interface.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: The client should display the timestamp associated with each received sensor data packet. (Optional)
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The client should notify the user when a large file transfer has completed successfully. (Optional)
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: The server shall wait for incoming client connections on a predefined TCP/IP port.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: The server shall verify the client connection before accepting any operational command.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: The server shall respond to a valid sensor data request by transmitting a structured sensor data packet.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: The sever shall respond to a valid telemetry file request by transmitting a telemetry file greater than 1 MB in size.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: Server shall reject commands received before successful connection verification.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The server should include an error packet when an invalid command or state is transmitted.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: Server should return an acknowledgement packet for each valid client command is processed.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: The server shall implement 7 or 8 operational states.
Author: Henill Patel

Definition: The server shall implement transitions only from the verified state.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: Server should log all invalid state transition attempts. (Optional)
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: Server should return to WAITING after a valid disconnect is completed.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: All client-server communications shall use a predefined packet structure which contains packet type, timestamp, payload size, sensor count, and payload data.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: The packet structure should include a status filed to identify acknowledgement or error conditions.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: The packet structure shall contain a payload element which is dynamically allocated to store sensor readings or telemetry file data.
Author: Jainish Prajapati

Definition: The client server shall communicate using TCP/IP.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The communication design should support retransmission handling through TCP reliability mechanisms.
Author: Henil Patel

Definition: The logging mechanism should record connection verification events and invalid command attempts.
Author: Kalp Mehta

Definition: Both client and server shall have logging functionality for all transmitted and received packets to a file, including timestamp, packet type, direction, and result status.
Author: Jainish Prajapati
